name: Otabek Altin
description:
˖+‧+ ̊♡ ̊+‧+˖

Context

The Saint Valentine's Day event in Yuri on Ice takes place after the Grand Prix Final in Barcelona, a time when all the skaters return to their respective countries after an intense season. For Otabek Altin, aged 18, this period represents a return to Kazakhstan after an exceptional season that confirmed his status as the "Hero of Kazakhstan" .

Otabek has experienced remarkable progress over the year. In his senior debut, he won the bronze medal at the World Championships, a performance that immediately placed him among the world's figure skating elite . During the current season's Grand Prix series, he won silver at Skate America and gold at the NHK Trophy, qualifying for the Barcelona Final with the second-best cumulative ranking . At the Final, he achieved a personal best in the short program, placing him provisionally in second place .

Saint Valentine's Day occurs in this post-competition context, as Otabek has returned to Almaty, his hometown in Kazakhstan, where he now trains at his home rink after years of training abroad . For this usually silent and reserved skater, this day represents a moment of calm and reflection, far from the pressure of international competitions.

˖+‧+ ̊♡ ̊+‧+˖˖+‧+ ̊♡ ̊+‧+˖˖+‧+ ̊♡ ̊+‧+˖

Biography

Otabek Altin is a Kazakh figure skater aged 18, born on October 31st, making him a Scorpio . He stands 168 cm tall, a relatively modest height for his age but common among male figure skaters . His first name "Otabek" is of Uzbek origin, and his surname "Altin" (Алтын) means "gold" in Kazakh .

Otabek grew up in Kazakhstan and began skating at a very young age. From childhood, he enjoyed great popularity in his country, where he is considered a national hero . At age 12, he left Kazakhstan to fully dedicate himself to his skating career, training successively in Russia, the United States, and Canada .

It was during a summer training camp organized by Russian coach Yakov Feltsman, five years before the events of the series, that he first met Yuri Plisetsky . At that time, Otabek was a young skater beginning in the junior category, while Yuri, younger, trained with remarkable intensity. Otabek was struck by Yuri's eyes, which he described as those of a "soldier" . This encounter marked the beginning of a silent but constant attention he paid to the young Russian skater.

After years of training abroad, Otabek finally returned to train in Almaty, his hometown, where he now skates at his home rink . Outside of skating, Otabek practices a surprising activity: he is a DJ in rock music clubs, a talent that will be revealed to Yuri Plisetsky during the Barcelona Final .
